chapter_title.ar: باب مَا لِلْمُطَلَّقَةِ الَّتِي لَمْ يُدْخَلْ بِهَا مِنَ الصَّدَاقِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 39 – What is for the divorced woman who has not been touched with, from the dower

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/183)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/183)
text: رَجُلٍ تَزَوَّجَ امْرَأَةً وَ أَمْهَرَهَا أَبَاهَا وَ قِيمَةُ أَبِيهَا خَمْسُمِائَةِ دِرْهَمٍ عَلَى أَنْ تُعْطِيَهُ أَلْفَ دِرْهَمٍ ثُمَّ طَلَّقَهَا قَبْلَ أَنْ يَدْخُلَ بِهَا قَالَ لَيْسَ عَلَيْهَا شَيْ‏ءٌ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad, from Ahmad, from Al Husayn Bin Saeed, from Al Nazar Bin Suwed, from Al Qasim Bin Suleyman, from Ubeyd Bin Zurara,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> regarding a man who married a woman, and set her father as her dower, and the value of her father was five hundred Dirhams upon that she would give him a thousand Dirhams. Then he divorced her before he slept with her. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘There is nothing upon her’.
en.sarwar: 10. Muhammad has narrated from Ahmad from al-Husayn ibn Sa‘id from al-Nadr ibn Suwayd from al-Qasim ibn Sulayman from ‘Ubayd ibn Zurarah who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the case of a man who marries a woman and sets her (slave) father as her mahr (dower). His price is five hundred dirham; then he divorces before going to bed with her.’ He (the Imam) said, ‘She does not owe anything to him.’”
